patch.value:
## Agent need
Maintainers need observability into how pm behaves in the field, but pm is git-native and privacy-sensitive, so no project data may leak.

## Delivered surface (pm-5oj5)
core/telemetry (consent-aware events, redaction); Sentry instrumentation; non-blocking OTLP queue + worker; pm health checks; telemetry stats (percentiles, error-rate, agent identity).

## Verified by
telemetry-env-var-docs and OTLP non-blocking tests. Enduring agent-value statement for the observability capability; new work lands in pm-5oj5.
As a maintainer, I want consent-aware local telemetry and health diagnostics, so that I can observe how pm is used and detect problems without leaking any project data

Telemetry is redacted and consent-gated (opt-in, never committed to git); OTLP export is non-blocking (queue + worker); pm health surfaces telemetry/extension/stack state; Sentry gating is enforced only when tokens are present.
Delivered and maintained by pm-5oj5: redacted consent-gated telemetry, non-blocking OTLP, health diagnostics, and token-gated Sentry; worker reconnect hardening tracked (pm-rj3w).
A maintainer observes usage and detects problems without leaking any data.
Redacted telemetry + non-blocking OTLP + health + Sentry gating shipped.
Delivered and maintained by pm-5oj5: redacted consent-gated telemetry, non-blocking OTLP, health diagnostics, and token-gated Sentry; worker reconnect hardening tracked (pm-rj3w).
